entry.title: Addressing Dictionary Issues with OSTR-Replicated Tables in SAP HANA Scale-Out Systems
entry.content:
Assumption: You Have Scaled Out SAP S/4HANA

Understanding Synchronous Table Replication

What and Why?

In a scale-out system, tables (or selected columns of column store tables) may be replicated across multiple hosts. This replication is essential to reduce network traffic, especially when frequently changing master data must be joined with other tables or partitions on different hosts.

Types of Table Replication

Synchronous vs. Asynchronous

Both asynchronous and synchronous table replication have their advantages and limitations. Below is a comparison of these two approaches:

Feature Optimistic Synchronous Table Replication (OSTR) Asynchronous Table Replication (ATR)
Advantages- Always consistent state between source and replica.
- No need for application developers to modify queries.
- Load balancing without changes to SQL.		- Lower overhead at the source node.
- Less impact on source transactions.		Disadvantages- Performance penalty during write transactions.- Asymmetry leads to potential outdated data on replicas.
- Developers must manage which queries can accept staleness.

For detailed insights, refer to the official SAP documentation here.

Recent Limitations and Fixes

Due to certain limitations, OSTR replication had to be disabled for specific tables during transport or structural changes. Users have encountered the following symptoms:

Error Message:

 

 
DBSL error 99 (db code 7): feature not supported: cannot perform statement when DDL AUTOCOMMIT OFF and table replication is turned on.

Solution

Recent SAP notes have addressed these issues. To ensure proper handling of DDL operations for tables with OSTR replication, follow these steps:

  1. Pre-requisites: Ensure you have the kernel patch provided with SAP Note 3456925 (DBSL procedure HDB_DBSL_CHECK_SCALEOUT).

  2. Implementation Steps:

    • Step 1: Stop the Application Server and back up the Kernel directory.
    • Step 2: Extract the LIB_DBSL.SAR file (as recommended in SAP Note 3456925).
    • Step 3: Restart the Application Server.
    • Step 4: Implement SAP Note 3486167 for the necessary corrections.

      By following these steps, you can successfully manage OSTR replication in your SAP S/4HANA scale-out environment, mitigating errors and ensuring smooth operations during table modifications.

entry.title: Activation of business functions for Waste and Recycling in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
​Waste & Recycling in SAP S/4HANA is an industry solution within Utilities (IS-U-WA) which helps waste management and recycling companies to manage waste in a smart and efficient way. It provides you with a wide-ranging customer care, service, logistics, and billing system that is fully integrated in a standard business software. Using this solution requires activation of certain business functions, this blog is aimed to act as a guide to illustrate the same.

​Business functions which are relevant for waste & recycling are –

  • ISU_UTIL_WASTE
  • ISU_WASTE_1
  • ISU_WASTE_2
  • ISU_WASTE_3
  • ISU_WASTE_4
  • ISU_WASTE_C&I_2
  • ISU_WASTE_RCI_2

Steps and Procedure to activate business functions - 

1. Log on SAP system with appropriate credentials. Ensure that you have necessary permissions to make changes to business functions.

2. Navigate to the business function activation. Use transaction SFW5 to access BF activation screen. This allowed you to view all available business functions and their statuses

3. Click continue when the below pop-up appears

4. Select the relevant business function set from the drop down box. Here choose "Utilities.

5. Select the checkbox in the planned state column of the business functions mentioned during the start of this guide. Ensure you understand each of the business functions and how it integrates with existing processes.

7. Choose "Activate Changes". The batch job for DDIC and BC set activation starts immediately. This job takes about 30-120 minutes roughly.

​Please Note: Refer the below SAP provided links for additional details:

SAP Help Portal - Utilities and Recycling 

SAP Help Portal  - Switching on Business Functions

entry.title: Planning plant and work center in execution order item
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management. To know more about this offering, please refer the blog:  Introduction to Service with Advanced Execution

This blog talks about planning plant and work center fields in Execution order item.

Planning plant and work center:

Planning plant and work center are available as additional input fields in the execution order item. These attributes are essential in maintenance management transactions for planning and executing maintenance activities. Therefore, these attributes have been included in the Execution order item.

These fields are available in assignment block of Execution Order Item Details under the section Maintenance:

                   

Typically, billable maintenance order is created as a follow-up from an execution order item. During this creation process, these fields are mandatory and would be transferred from the execution order item to the maintenance order. Thus, they must be entered in the execution order item before creating the corresponding maintenance order.

There is a mapping between the planning plant and the service organization, as well as between the work center and the service team. Service organization and service team can be treated as counterparts of planning plant and work center.

Defaulting the Planning plant and Work center:

There is a defaulting mechanism for planning plant and work center in the Service transaction. Defaulting can work only with the legacy organizational management. This does not work with Enterprise organizational management.

The planning plant and work center can be defaulted from Service organization unit/Service organization and Service Employee group respectively, if the appropriate mapping is maintained.

Service organization unit always takes the first precedence in the defaulting of planning plant.

The planning plant and work center can also be entered and updated by the user manually.

To enable defaulting in legacy organizational management, we need to maintain the following business attributes for the appropriate organizational units as required:

Organizational unit

Attribute Description

Technical Attribute Name

Service organization

R/3 maintenance planning plant

R3_IHWRK

Service team

Work Center Object ID

WORKCENTER

Changeability:

Planning plant is allowed to be changed until the creation of the corresponding maintenance order, i.e., until the execution status is set to Execution Planning. Once Maintenance order is linked to the item, planning plant becomes read-only.

Similarly, work center is allowed to be changed until the Technical Completion of the maintenance order, i.e, until the execution status is set to Execution Done. Any changes in work center is transferred to the corresponding maintenance order. Once execution status is set to Execution Done, work center becomes read-only.

The work center can be changed on the Maintenance order as well and in this case, the work center update will reflect on the Execution order item also.

Validations:

There are certain validations implemented on planning plant and work center fields, as below:

  1. Plant vs Planning plant:-

         Planning plant must be explicitly entered in the following customizing activity:

         <spro path>

         <spro customizing entry>

     2. Planning plant vs Sales Organization and Distribution channel

         Plant must be assigned to the Sales Organization and Distribution Channel in the following customizing activity:

         <SPRO Path>        

         <SPRO mapping>

     3. Planning plant vs Sales organization (Intercompany check)

         Planning plant and Sales organization must be assigned to the same company code.

         <SPRO Path>

         <Plant and Company code mapping>     

         <Sales org and company code mapping>

     4. Planning plant vs Maintenance order type

         The proposed Maintenance Order Type must be assigned to the planning plant.

         <SPRO path>

         <SPRO customizing entry for Order type and planning plant mapping>

     5. Planning Plant vs Work Center Plant

         Planning Plant company code and Work Center plant company code must be assigned to same controlling area.

         <SPRO Path>

         <Plant and Company Code mapping>

         <Controlling Area and Company Code mapping>                                                                         

     6. Planning plant vs Main Reference object

         Planning plant company code and main reference object company code must be assigned to the same controlling area.

         <SPRO Path>

         <Plant and Company Code mapping> 

         <Reference object company code>

         <Controlling Area and Company Code mapping>

entry.title: Why S/4HANA Extensibility Options are crucial.
entry.content:
I have worked for almost 30 years in the SAP ERP space and have seen many innovations in SAP software. Although there have been many changes during those years, the challenges in my job remain almost the same. As a developer, enterprise architect, SAP mentor, speaker, teacher, and advisor, I ask myself: ‘How can I add additional value for my customers out of their ERP system?’. The answer is always the same: Focus on the customer's unique requirements because SAP will always be a best-practice solution.

With this answer in mind, I researched SAP's strategy for the public edition of S/4HANA Cloud. I found that SAP thought about the customer's unique requirements and not only to help their customers. SAP’s plan to become a cloud company without losing the current customer base, which runs the ERP on-premise, was the most significant driver. Moving to the cloud for the current customer base is not that easy due to the high investments for their customers due to many custom enhancements for their current running business systems. And SAP devised a solution to overcome these customers’ needs: S/4HANA Extensibility Options.

This prompts me to rephrase my question: ‘How can I get more value from S/4HANA with SAP Extensibility options? ‘I will try to answer this question in this article series.

In Part 1, I will explain why SAP Extensibility options are critical to SAP strategy and why customers benefit from this capability.

In Part 2, I will discuss these extension possibilities in more depth.

In part 3, I will explain which option is best for third-party cloud vendors and SAP add-on solution providers to integrate their solutions with S/4HANA for the long term. 

 

Introduction

From the early days, SAP customers built extensions on top of their SAP ERP systems, which added a lot of value but also caused IT departments a lot of headaches. For years, SAP has preached going for the SAP standard applications without modifications and implementing company templates, which should be rolled out to other departments and companies.

However, this will not bring the value customers expect, and often, this frustrates the use of SAP products after the companies roll out their templates. And there is a good reason why this approach fails.

When software vendors start building a solution, their software will only deliver solutions for situations that fit the current needs. However, a software vendor also wants to create solutions that fit multiple customers, and this will always result in generic solutions, which will mainly become a best-practice solution over time. SAP tries to overcome the generic part by providing customizing and configuration capabilities in the software.  But in most cases, this is not enough to fulfill the customers' requirements, and there will be a gap. This gap can be solved by adding third-party add-ons, developing custom enhancements, integrating the application with other applications, or just downsizing the scope. However, when customers keep the unsupported requirements to a minimum and start only with standard SAP processes during the SAP implementation project, the number of extensions will grow after going live. Companies' support and development departments will gradually add extensions based on additional functional requirements.

And there is another good reason why a company-wide standard SAP template approach will never be suitable. If you look at an end-to-end business process in a company, you will see that it will be different when multiple departments or divisions are involved. In one country, one department will work with a few people. In other countries, the same business processes are split over multiple departments with multiple people involved. Government rules, work ethic, and the state of automation can also significantly impact.

Furthermore, the level of automation and the use of artificial intelligence (AI) can significantly influence business processes.  Most business processes in companies start with non-automated and unstructured steps, some requiring detailed software solutions and some extending beyond the company's boundaries. This requires flexible and open software within the company, which can easily integrate with other internal and external software products. This capability is increasingly crucial as vendors transition their solutions to the cloud.

SAP cloud-first approach

This means that SAP ERP software, like S/4HANA, should be open and flexible on the one hand but also robust and stable on the other. Even more critical, innovations should be applied quickly without disturbing the current business. This forces SAP to change its approach to provide SAP software to its customers in a way that will fulfill these capabilities. The SAP cloud-first approach is directly a result of this.

For on-premise software, SAP is enormously dependent on its customers’ upgrade strategy, but in the cloud, SAP can control its product lifecycle and the needed innovation. Now that artificial intelligence has become increasingly important, the state of the active software and access to the data is crucial.  This is why SAP wants to run the S/4HANA software in an SAP-controlled data center and strongly encourages customers to move from on-premise SAP ECC and S/4HANA into the S/4HANA Cloud.   

SAP has used this cloud-first approach for a few years but should have explained its goals and strategy more precisely to its customers. During time, most business processes in SAP ECC become complex and are running by centralized departments. But with the cloud everybody is connected, and new cloud companies saw an opportunity to decentralized and extend these business processes with new innovative features. They introduced a lot of new cloud application in the enterprise market for specific domains like human resources, sales and marketing, procurement, and planning and reporting were introduced.

This was a thread for SAP and SAP also entered this cloud application market by acquire companies like SuccessFactors, Ariba, Concur and Hybris. These cloud solutions could run in their own domain, but to be competitive in the cloud ERP market, SAP needs to smoothly integrate them with their ERP offering.

To prevent overlapping functionality and aligning the data models of the different cloud solutions, it forces SAP to change the data model of their ERP system and even decouple and remove specific processes known from the SAP ECC system. SAP presented S/4HANA as the successor to SAP ECC, but in reality, it provided less functionality than its customers expected from their ERP system.

The first S/4HANA Cloud edition was mainly built around financial processes. Only the core functionality of other business processes was available in S/4HANA when it was needed for finance-related reasons. Many core processes of SAP ECC are now offered as additional SAP cloud offerings, like SuccessFactors, Ariba, Concur, Analytics Cloud, and SAP Sales Cloud.

S/4HANA Cloud public edition

This split of functionality also greatly impacted SAP's delivery strategy. SAP must integrate the new cloud offerings with its S/4HANA system to provide a seamless ERP experience for its portfolio. And this isn’t that easy. Besides connecting these systems, they also need to align the user interfaces and, even more importantly, the different business models of the products. For this reason, the S/4HANA data model must be simplified, and unnecessary code and configuration should be removed. This is done by limiting the configuration options by only supporting predefined best practices and restricting the development capabilities. S/4HANA Cloud public edition was born.

 

Figure 1- SAP Extensibility Options

With the simplification of S/4HANA Cloud public edition, the gap between customer requirements and standard offered functionality becomes more significant. Due to the limited scope of configurable processes and the limited integration and custom development capabilities, the move to S/4HANA Cloud public edition was much behind SAP’s expectations.

 

SAP S/4HANA Key User Extensibility

Since the first versions of this S/4HANA Cloud public edition, you can use some SAP Fiori apps to adapt your S/4HANA system. You can add and hide fields to standard SAP UIs and influence the logic in some predefined places by adding code and activating business add-ins (Badi’s). You can also create custom business objects with a UI and expose them as API. This capability in S/4HANA is now known as SAP S/4HANA Key User Extensibility, but the possibilities of this extensibility option are limited adapting S/4HANA standard functionality.

Side-by-Side Extensibility

SAP addressed this and came up with a solution. SAP provides the Business Technology Platform (BTP) for more complex applications and third-party integration with S/4HANA. The platform offers different capabilities, approaches, and solutions for building customer applications, workflows, and integration scenarios by accessing APIs and Events between BTP and the S/4HANA environment. This capability in S/4HANA is now known as Side-by-Side Extensibility. In Side-by-Side scenarios, you can develop your custom applications in any language or tooling the BTP platform provides. Depending on your needs and skills, you can use low-code tools like SAP Build Apps and SAP Cloud Integration or pro-code tools like SAP Build Code or an ABAP environment on SAP BTP. Side-by-Side applications can be made available in the S/4HANA Cloud standard Launchpad, so users do not know they switch to BTP and back again.

But Side-by-Side Extensibility has its downsides. The data exchanged between S/4HANA and BTP should be available through released APIs or business events. But not all processes have APIs or business events available yet. Also, big loads or high volumes could become a bottleneck in this approach. SAP tried to solve this bottleneck by replicating S/4HANA data to the BTP platform and vice versa, but this solution became too complex and wasn’t a working solution to be rolled out to all customers.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Developer extensibility

The gap for building applications that are too complex to be built with the SAP S/4HANA Key User extensibility or are too tightly connected to core S/4HANA data to be built with side-by-side extensibility is now solved by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Developer extensibility. Developers can create tightly coupled applications on the same ABAP runtime and HANA database used the embedded ABAP environment of the S/4HANA Cloud system.

ABAP Cloud

This looks like the old days in SAP ECC when customers could build any application, but this is untrue. To become robust and stable in S/4HANA, SAP and its customers' code bases are strictly separated. The two can only interact with each other by accessing released data objects like ABAP CDS, using released ABAP classes and functions, and using a restricted set of ABAP commands, together known as ABAP Cloud and following SAP’s Clean Core strategy.

However, moving to a Clean Core S/4HANA system isn’t that easy, and SAP launched two sales programs to help their existing and new customers move to the Cloud:

  • Grow with SAP
  • RISE with SAP

 

 

Figure 2 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud offering

Grow with SAP

SAP launched ‘Grow with SAP’ for new customers who want to implement from scratch. These customers can do a greenfield SAP implementation and support their processes based on the S/4HANA Cloud public edition and other solutions in the SAP Cloud portfolio. 

RISE with SAP

However, moving to S/4HANA and other cloud products isn’t easy for existing SAP ECC customers. As described before, there isn’t a one-to-one fit, and these customers primarily invested a lot in custom code and third-party solutions and run processes on custom developments and configurations SAP isn’t supporting anymore in the S/4HANA Cloud public edition. A big-bang move to SAP Cloud ERP is risky, and a greenfield implementation is too expensive. However, SAP doesn’t want to lose these customers; instead, SAP intends to move them to SAP's future strategy with a golden path. For this, SAP launched ‘RISE with SAP,’ which will eventually help these customers move their on-premise systems to the SAP cloud. SAP Rise uses the S/4HANA Cloud private edition. The S/4HANA code base of this version is more in line with the classic SAP ECC code base. This allows customers to implement brownfield and upgrade their SAP ECC to S/4HANA. With this approach, customers will still profit from their past investments. Investments in configurations, custom code, and third-party products will be supported as much as possible, which minimizes the risk and the implementation costs. Customers can directly profit from the S/4HANA innovations and use ABAP Cloud for new developments. Customers and third-party addon partners will also get the time to migrate the current classic ABAP custom developments to ABAP Cloud. It will give SAP the time to add capabilities and functionality to the S/4HANA Cloud public edition, so there will be a smooth S/4HANA Cloud transition from the private edition to the public edition in the future.

Conclusion

As described in this article, the capability of integrating with other systems, building custom code, and using third-party solutions was crucial in the past. It will be even more critical when customers move their applications to the cloud. For modern business processes, companies and business partners must work closer together and their systems must be connected. As a result, business applications should also be open and flexible. SAP recognizes this need and intensively supports it by providing multiple ways to build extensions to its cloud products.

 

Figure 3 – S/4HANA detailed extensibility options

I recognized eight options for S/4HANA combined with BTP supported by low-code and pro-code development tools. In my next article, I will explore these options more in-depth from a customer's point of view and will tell which Extensibility Options fit the best for different use cases.

This article was originally posted on the linked-in profile of Robert Eijpe

entry.title: ASUG Best Practices for Industries Event Oct 2024
entry.content:
The ASUG Best Practices Events for Industrial Manufacturing happened in Houston, USA, from Oct 14-16. The event was attended by SAP customers from multiple industries, including industrial manufacturing. There were multiple customer best practices sharing sessions and keynotes around Cloud ERP and AI. 

Here are the key takeaways

1. Need for speed
Cloud ERP's value is now a given. The focus has shifted from "Why Cloud ERP" to "How" and "How quickly." Companies are pushing for faster ERP implementations to stay competitive. The days of 7 year long ERP transformation business case are long gone. The board has no appetite for such long term projects, given the dynamic nature of businesses and disruptions today.

Faster implementations, faster value, faster speed to market are defining characteristics of the modern day ERP implementations. 

2. Need for higher ROI

Companies starting Cloud ERP projects with a "clean core" strategy & execution are getting higher ROI. Rapid adoption of innovations and meeting market demand are driving success stories. I did a session with the VP of IT applications and CIO of an industrial customer who have two S/4HANA Cloud landscapes. One of them was driven through clean core thinking from the start and other was just a landscape conversion from old ERP to S/4HANA Cloud.

The one with clean core thinking was obviously the winner in terms of ROI, helping the customer in launching new innovations in the market within a 90 day turnaround time. 

More details on References on sap.com  

3. Need for superior user experience
Embedded AI is not just about boosting productivity, it makes organizations more attractive to the new generation of employees, who are used to superior user experiences from the consumer applications. You cannot imagine retaining younger talent with a complex system coded 20 years back. The UX is key. 

Most customers I spoke to were curious about implementing Joule to utilize the insights synthesized by Joule agents operating on the business data within SAP systems, unlocking not just productivity but a superlative user experience. 

Finally a picture to wrap up a great event with an amazing set of colleagues from across the world!

entry.title: SAP Product Inspiration Series for Industries & Intelligent CX (Wave #5)
entry.content:
Hi everyone! Welcome to Wave 5 of our Product Inspiration Video Series covering the latest innovations of SAP Intelligent CX along with S/4HANA, Business AI, Joule, BTP and industry-tailored solutions. Please find the latest deep dive videos from our product teams below.

 

1. Order Taking in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 in Action

Priyanka Bhat from the Sales Cloud Product Management at SAP provides an overview on Order Capturing. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the broad set of capabilities of Order Capturing, Simulation and Pricing in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 along with SAP S/4HANA as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio from SAP.

The key highlights shared in this video are:

entry.title: Copilot-driven Simulation of Non-SAP Systems and EDI Partners for SAP S/4HANA programs
entry.content:
Why This Article is Essential

In SAP Greenfield S/4HANA transformations, efficient simulation of external, often hard-to-reach non-SAP systems and EDI partners is a game-changer. By using service virtualization in combination with a Copilot—a smart AI-driven assistant—SAP teams can now accelerate and streamline the development (DEV) and integration (SIT) phases more effectively. Solutions like Int4 Suite, crafted specifically for SAP environments, enable developers to simulate third-party systems and EDI partners effortlessly. But with the added capability of an AI Copilot, they can further leverage historical data, navigate test cases, and simulate extensive partner systems swiftly—all in a natural language interface. As per Figure 1 - from millions of available test cases we can select only those which we need to simulate for manual testing of an SAP Greenfield implementation (or validate in case we can automate some testing already) and run all of them in a matter of seconds without having any access to the 3rd party systems or EDI partner landscapes. 

 

Figure 1 - simulating EDI partners from the copilot using Int4 Suite  

Key Benefits of Using AI Copilot in Greenfield SAP S/4HANA Transformations

Effortless Historical Data Access:
With a Copilot integrated into the SAP environment, historical messages, which serve as key test cases, can be loaded into SAP's vector database. We can use Int4 Robotic Crawler to download millions of historical messages as shown in Video 1. 

Video 1 - loading historical electronic messages to the SAP HANA Vector Engine 

This allows users to retrieve relevant test messages in real-time using simple, natural language queries, such as “Show test messages for vendor A” or “Find previous EDI transactions.” By searching in plain language, SAP developers and functional consultants save valuable time compared to traditional methods.

Boosted Simulation Capabilities:
Service virtualization, enhanced with AI Copilot functionality, facilitates faster simulation of third-party systems and EDI partners across various SAP S/4HANA tiers. By leveraging historical messages and simulation frameworks, project teams can operate at a pace significantly faster than before, especially when compared to manually requesting or recreating each test scenario individually. Copilot also speaks all languages as per Figure 2. 

Figure 2 - Simulations and validations work in all languages 

Overcoming Common Greenfield Transformation Challenges

Inaccessible Third-Party Systems and EDI Partners:
Many third-party systems and EDI partners may be unavailable or not yet configured for SAP Greenfield projects, causing delays. The AI Copilot, paired with service virtualization, provides a dynamic workaround by simulating these unavailable systems instantly and at scale, letting projects move forward without depending on external readiness. Copilot would resent historical electronic messages simulating the 3rd party systems and EDI partners as shown in Figure 3.

Figure 3 - Copilot can either only simulate messages going to SAP S/4HANA or also validate them. 

Reducing Manual Mockups:

Traditionally, mockup creation is a time-intensive task, especially for outbound messages like IDOCs or ABAP proxies. The Copilot automates much of this by retrieving historical message templates or generating new, reusable ones, eliminating delays caused by manual mockup processes.

The Role of AI Copilot in SAP S/4HANA Transformation Programs

An AI Copilot enhances the effectiveness of service virtualization in several ways:

  • SAP Functional Consultants:
    By streamlining the search and retrieval of test messages within SAP, consultants can quickly simulate and validate business processes directly in the system without waiting on external systems to be ready, keeping projects on schedule.

  • ABAP and Interface Developers:
    Copilot enables the seamless capture and reuse of messages from other environments, whether production or test, for use in development. This automation expedites the interface testing process by providing instant access to relevant data from other landscapes.

  • Project and Release Managers:
    Copilot assists with test cycle coordination by managing and simulating multiple third-party systems, reducing the number of dependent applications and allowing project teams to focus on core SAP functionalities.

  • Testing Teams:
    AI Copilot helps teams zero in on SAP S/4HANA testing by handling simulations for third-party components. This minimizes the need for extensive, manual third-party tests and allows testing to proceed smoothly.

Leveraging Copilot-Driven Simulation for Cost and Time Efficiency

Reduced Testing Time

Using Copilot to guide simulations during DEV and SIT phases results in faster testing cycles, as third-party system dependencies are alleviated through swift, AI-facilitated simulations.

Significant Cost Savings

By simulating the landscape of non-SAP systems through Copilot, SAP S/4HANA projects can significantly cut down on costs associated with setting up, configuring, and maintaining various systems across multiple testing environments. These savings contribute not only to the bottom line but also to project sustainability.

Early Defect Detection

Copilot supports extensive test runs by readily providing relevant test cases, enabling early identification of potential issues, which reduces production costs and minimizes the risks of operational disruptions later on.

Optimizing SAP S/4HANA Transformations with Historical Data in a Vector Database

Historical data and message logs, stored in an SAP S/4HANA vector database, become powerful assets in testing. By loading these past messages, SAP teams can directly access previous transactions and replicate them as needed through the Copilot. This approach eliminates redundant testing steps and delivers tailored test cases on demand, ensuring quicker response times and fewer bottlenecks.

Maximizing Simulation Impact in Greenfield SAP S/4HANA Projects

While simulations are traditionally beneficial for regression testing during UAT, the introduction of Copilot-driven simulations shines most during early stages like DEV and SIT. By implementing Copilot-assisted simulations early, project teams bypass the challenges posed by unavailable third-party systems, achieve higher test quality, and accelerate the Greenfield SAP S/4HANA transformation process.

Learning More About Copilot and Service Virtualization for SAP S/4HANA Transformations

Explore the synergy of AI Copilot and service virtualization with in-depth resources like the OpenSAP/SAP Learning course, “Avoid SAP S/4HANA Project Delays with Third-Party Systems Service Virtualization.” This course covers the fundamentals of service virtualization, its optimal use cases, and how it integrates with tools like Copilot to boost SAP S/4HANA project efficiency while minimizing environmental impact.

entry.title: PDM Newsletter RISE with SAP - November 2024
entry.content:
Welcome to the PDM Newsletter RISE with SAP Blog! 

We're excited to introduce you to our November newsletter, your go-to resource for staying connected with the latest innovations, insights, and trends in the RISE with SAP ecosystem. Each edition is thoughtfully curated to provide exclusive expert guidance, inspiring stories, and actionable content designed to help you enhance your skills and stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP.

Whether you're looking to explore emerging technologies or gain valuable knowledge tailored to your needs, our newsletter has you covered. We also encourage you to share this resource with your network and become an active member of our vibrant partner community.

Thank you for your continued support! Should you have any feedback or questions, don’t hesitate to reach out. We’re here to help you make the most of your RISE with SAP journey.

Stay informed, and sign up today! 

 

S/4HANA Cloud  - Private Edition                                                                                                                                                                            

Top Highlights

Overview

Register/Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | 2023 FPS02 Release – Part 1

 

SAP has released the second “Feature Pack Stack” (FPS), SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for the 2023 release, on October 09, 2024. This is the second in a sequence of three FPS that will be delivered over two years and introduce new optional capabilities. Once again, it comes up with a new set of capabilities bringing Artificial Intelligence (AI), simplification, innovation, automation, analytics, and user experience to customers. We delivered more than 280 innovations and enhancements in this FPS02!

 

Access

 

 

 

Launch of the Elevate Excellence Program

Our customers are placing the future of their businesses in our hands. They trust world-class SAP technology to deliver tangible value and rely on our partners to make this a reality by providing: Specialist SAP technical skills , Local knowledge and understanding High quality engagements and consulting services , Business process and industry expertise.

 

Access      

Coming in 2025: Exciting New Updates for the SAP PartnerEdge Program

As part of our ongoing commitment to improve your partner experience, we’re delivering new enhancements to the SAP PartnerEdge program. To learn more about the updates due to launch in January 2025, including the new Development Fund, visit SAP Partner Portal and read the notice sent on October 1, 2024.

 

Access 

 

 

      

RISE with SAP Partner Activation Kit

Access the latest RISE with SAP marketing and enablement resources to showcase the value of our solutions to your customers.

Access

 

 

Starter system for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition:
Getting Started Guide

This guide provides information about the Starter system for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, referred to as “system” in this guide. It is a standardized system with the core scope of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition containing demo data, SAP Best Practices and configuration. When you log in you can to start working with demo instructions and also configure the system according to your needs.

 

 Access

 

      

Events and Webinars for Partners

Overview

Register/Access

RISE into the Future / Virtual experience

 

Watch the event replay to explore the new AI capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Supply Chain, enabled by Joule.

 

Access

 

 

SAP Discovery Day for HCM, 18. November 2024

 

Wie sieht die Zukunft der Personalverwaltung und -entwicklung aus?

Registrieren Sie sich jetzt für den virtuellen Demo-Tag! Unter dem Motto „Make every employee a success story.”, erleben Sie in praxisnahen Demos, wie SAP SuccessFactors Ihr Unternehmen transformieren kann. Tauchen Sie ein in die Welt innovativer HR-Lösungen und entdecken Sie, wie Sie schon heute die Talente von morgen fördern können.

Das Besondere: Sie entscheiden, welche Session sie interessant finden! Melden Sie sich für den Slot an, in dem Sie SAP SuccessFactors näher kennenlernen möchten.

 

Access

 

SAP Expert Coaching: Accelerate Your SAP BTP Use Case

Receive individualized coaching services for SAP Business Technology Platform and allow SAP experts to offer their expertise for your specific use case. Delve into this article for more information and to see the additional services available to you.

Access

 

Partner Certification Academies for SAP Business Technology Platform

Your learning path will be enhanced and accelerated by working directly with SAP experts who will guide you through the content of selected SAP BTP learning journeys and help you prepare to pass the associated certification exams.

.

Access

 

 

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition – byte size monthly sessions for Partner Cloud Architects

You are invited to this monthly session to cover any key technical or delivery SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition topics which will help you better carry out your role.

 

 Register

 

  • Session: 12th November 2024
    10:00 AM to 11:00 AM CET
  • Session: 12th November 2024
    04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CET

Master Data Management with SAP

 

During the session the partner participants will learn more on master data management with SAP solutions.

 

Register

 

  • Session: 15th  November 2024
    10:00 AM to 11:00 AM CEST

Quality and efficiency of the partner CEE role - what are impacting factors

 

The session will begin with an introduction to the Partner CEE's responsibilities, followed by an in-depth discussion of the main factors that impact their effectiveness. A practical, "practice-based" approach will be presented to address these challenges, alongside ISSI’s Customer Success service offerings. Participants will also gain insights from experience-sharing, reference sites, and best practices, with a focus on actionable next steps to enhance partner success

 

  Register

 

  • Session: 5th  November 2024
    10:00 AM to 11:00 AM CET
  • Session: 5th November 2024 04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CET

 

Experience SAP Build in action end-to-end hands-on technical academy

 

Join us for a multi-part virtual series where you will experience how to use the SAP Build portfolio

 

    Register

 

What's new in SAP Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2

Update on the latest enhancements to SAP Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA on-premise/SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

 

    Register

 

  • Session: 19th  September 2024
    04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CEST

Updates on SAP Datasphere – AI, Semantic Onboarding

 

During the session the partner participants will learn more on the last updates for SAP Datasphere: AI, Semantic Onboarding

 

   Register

 

  • Session: 22th November 2024
    10:00 AM to 11:00 AM CEST

 

 

 

Business AI

Overview

Register/Access

Provisioning and Implementing Joule - Learning Journey

This learning journey offers a comprehensive approach to understanding, configuring, and integrating Joule, SAP’s AI copilot, across various SAP platforms, including SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. It will guide you through both the functional aspects of leveraging Joule to boost productivity for your customers and the technical configurations needed to set it up effectively.

 

   Access

SAP AI Core

SAP AI Core helps you to develop and productize AI functions with out-of-the-box integration with SAP solutions.

   Access

 

Generative AI Hub

The generative AI hub incorporates generative AI into your AI activities in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

   Access

 

AI in Retail – Redefining the Future

Discover how retailers invest in technology to thrive in the era of omnichannel commerce. This campaign describes in four chapters how retailers are bolstering customer centricity, championing sustainability, fostering innovation, and enhancing operational efficiency by making informed decisions through data analysis and effectively navigating the intricate dynamics of the retail landscape.

   Access

 

 

AI Essentials for Partners

This monthly update features SAP’s latest AI announcements, enablement, and events – right at your fingertips. Explore top highlights and delve deeper into the finer details broken into different categories of interest. Stay tuned for more updates.

   Access

 

 

 

Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide

 

We are excited to introduce the latest update to our SAP AI capabilities and add a new feature to our SAP Business.

 

   Access

AI and digital modalities for partners

 

At SAP, digital modalities are innovative tools and technologies that boost productivity, shorten sales cycles, and enhance customer experiences. Through the "AI and digital modalities for partners" webinar, SAP partners will learn how to leverage these tools and best practices to accelerate demand generation with SAP.

 

   Register

 

  • Session: 14th  November 2024
    11:00 AM to 12:00 PM Europe

 

SAP Business AI scenario activation

 

Please join us in this session to learn from SAP experts about managing and activating embedded SAP Business AI scenarios.

 

    Register

 

  • Session 1: 7th  November 2024
    10:00 AM to 11:00 AM CET
  • Session 2: 7th  September 2024
    04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CET

 

L100 SAP Business AI and RISE with SAP

 

 

L1 presentation for sales employees, aimed at customers who use SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA On-Premise. The content is about the benefits of SAP Business AI in RISE with SAP, which use cases and innovations are planned.

 

   Access

RISE with SAP Transformation

Overview

Register/Access

Transitioning from an SAP ERP - S/4HANA On-Premise Practitioner to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Practitioner – A Guide for Skill Conversion.

 

This blog serves as a guide, outlining recommendations and strategies that you can adopt to transition smoothly and quickly. It leverages your business expertise from an on-premise environment to the public cloud.

 

   Access

PartnerEdge page for Learning Managers

 

Below are resources that we have created for the learning managers within our partner ecosystem to help understand the change, have a clear picture of where your consultants are in the process and resources to help you navigate this transition.

 

   Access

Competency & Specialization

 

The competency framework evolves SAP’s recognition of its partner ecosystem expertise by categorizing partners by the competency and specialization they offer the market giving customers transparency on the skills, expertise, and maturity of a partner’s SAP practice. The competencies and specializations are aligned to SAP’s cloud portfolio and are cloud-focused.

 

   Access

Unlock the secret to successful RISE with SAP Cross LoB selling and enjoy increased revenues, profits, and customer loyalty

 

With the surge in demand for RISE with SAP, now is the moment for our partners to improve their ability to drive larger deal sizes that can deliver higher value to customers. This is where the cross line of business topic becomes even more important.
Join this enablement session to learn how the SAP Customer Evolution cross-solution area approach, resources, and tools, along with the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite will elevate your sales engagements to a new level of productivity.

 

  Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS02– What’s New

 

We have recently released the latest updates for SAP EWM with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 2 update. The enhancements we deliver not only further improve and compliment the SAP EWM features of the SAP S/4HANA 2023 release, but also expand integration options for EWM, such as integration with Service Order Management and extended Service Parts Planning, streamline processing steps for the Next Generation Just-In-Time (nJIT) external replenishment when using embedded S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management, Private Edition , enable circular logistic flows for returnable and reusable packaging material, offer new field extensibility options for select Warehouse Monitor nodes, and much more. 

 

   Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | 2023 FPS02 Release – Part 1

 

SAP has released the second “Feature Pack Stack” (FPS), RISE in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for the 2023 release, on October 09, 2024. This second sequence of three FPS will be delivered over two years and introduce new optional capabilities. Once again, it comes up with a new set of capabilities bringing Artificial Intelligence (AI), simplification, innovation, automation, analytics, and user experience to customers. We delivered more than 280 innovations and enhancements in this FPS02!

 

    Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2023 Product Tour

 

It is this time of the year again! SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2023 release is here. It is my pleasure to guide you through a product tour on the 9th wave of innovations available for our private cloud customers.

 

    Access

SAP Introduces a New Validation Recognition for Service Partners

To support customers’ RISE with SAP transformations, SAP is training and validating partners to use RISE with SAP Methodology and collaborating closely with these partners on quality and consistency. By following the methodology, customers can drive predictable timelines for implementations and provide progress transparency with key milestone assessments.

 

    Access

Customer Success Excellence Hub

 

Customer success is partner success. SAP and partners together delivering customer success. Customer success is all about creating the best customer experience – supporting customers to continuously realize value from their SAP cloud investment, delivering a simple consistent experience across SAP and partners. Customer success is a key part of everyone’s role – from sales through to service and support. Investing in customer success is an investment in growth.

 

    Access

SAP BTP Guidance Framework – Episode 1: What’s in for architects?

We are pleased to announce a dedicated webinar series to bring you closer to the SAP BTP Guidance Framework.

 

    Register

 

  • Session 1: 13th  November 2024
    11:00 AM to 12:00 PM Americas
  • Session 2: 14th  November 2024
    09:00 AM to 10:00 AM CET

News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, RISE with SAP, and more

We are pleased to announce the availability of the updates to SAP Activate. This release significantly enhances the methodology guidance across a wide range of products and solution-specific content. These updates aim to streamline and improve the implementation process, providing a more seamless experience for our valued users. Here are the key highlights for this release. You can learn more in the detailed sections below.

 

 

     Access

RISE with SAP: Qualification and recognition for partners

Enhance your skills, increase your presence, and solidify your credibility in the marketplace by achieving designation and recognition available to SAP partners who deliver implementation services for RISE with SAP.

 

     Access

SAP Private Cloud Metering

SAP Private Cloud Metering provides full transparency on the SAP Private Cloud consumption with monthly updated metering data. The metering data is made available in SAP for Me so that license gaps can be identified and clarified in a timely and product-specific manner.

 

     Access

SAP S/4HANA Customer Evolution Program

 

The SAP S/4HANA Customer Evolution Program is designed to help our ERP installed base customers accelerate their move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It comprises an end-to-end methodology providing prescriptive guidance, best practices, and automated tools and more to simplify our customer’s transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

     Access

Transition Brownfield Projects

This Knowledge Base Document is the central reference to additional documents focused on guidelines and information for Brownfield Projects (Migration/ Conversion or System Copy) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

 

      Access

      

RISE with SAP adoption framework

 

Use this pitch deck to present RISE with SAP adoption framework to your customers. The RISE with SAP adoption framework provides clarity on the entire cloud journey and brings partners and SAP together to collaborate under a single cloud operational governance model to proactively manage our common success.

 

 

    Access

 

Customer Onboarding Webcast

Select the solution of interest to access live and on demand webinars tailored to simplify your journey with SAP.

  • SAP Cloud ERP
  • SAP BTP
  • SAP SuccessFactors HCM
  • SAP Financial Management
  • SAP Training and Adoption
  • SAP Signavio and LeanIX
  • SAP Intelligent Spend and Business Network
  • SAP Supply Chain Management

    Access

 

 

Signavio

Overview

Register/Access

 SAP Signavio for governance, risk and compliance – What is new? How to best approach the topic?

 

Partners and customers can substantially improve their operational efficiency and risk mitigation with SAP Signavio. In this webinar we want to give you the latest updates and outline how to approach the topic well in a project and benefit from SAP Signavio.

 

Register

 

  • Session: 27th   November 2024
    10:00 AM to 11:00 AM CET

Onboarding Resource Center: One-Stop-Shop for SAP Signavio

 

If you are a new SAP Signavio customer, it can be overwhelming to find your bearings around the variety of SAP websites, documentation and platforms. With the huge number of texts, videos and other materials available on SAP Signavio, it can be hard to find the right information. If you can find it at all.  That’s the reason we have created the Onboarding Resource Center (ORC), to give you a single access point to find all relevant resources and information about SAP Signavio – well-structured and at a glance. 

 

 Access

Mastering SAP Signavio - Best practices for partnering with SAP Signavio

Join us for an in-depth webinar where we explore proven strategies for effectively partnering with SAP Signavio. Learn best practices for engaging with the SAP Signavio team, leveraging commercial opportunities, and navigating diverse routes to market. We will provide practical, real-world insights tailored to various partner profiles, offering actionable takeaways that will empower you to enhance your sales efforts and drive success in the field.

 

 Register

 

  • Session 1: 05th  November 2024
    06:00 AM to 07:00 AM CET
  • Session 2: 06th  November 2024
    05:00 PM to 06:00 PM CET

Value case for SAP Signavio - way forward and Q&A

This webinar is a follow up to the webinar on the SAP Signavio value methodology. In addition to the earlier content and a quick refresher you will get insights into the SAP Signavio value case going forward. We also outline how SAP Signavio works with partners on supporting them in the customer specific value case approach

Register

 

  • Session 1: 05th  November 2024
    09:00  to 10:00  JST
  • Session 2: 05th  November 2024
    04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CET

How to create a compelling value case with SAP Signavio (hands-on guide)

SAP partners are often looking for more compelling ways to create a strong value case, notably in pre-studies, but also in other contexts. In this hands-on session we present a proven methodology on how to create a strong and very customer specific value case aided by SAP Signavio. This methodology is being used by many partners already and has been honed over time to ideally support partners. We will outline the prerequisites and guide you through the whole process

.

 

Register

 

  • Session 1: 04th  November 2024
    06:00 AM  to 08:00 AM  CET
  • Session 2: 04th  November 2024
    04:00 PM to 06:00 PM CET

Explore SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager is a cloud-based process transformation solution that gives companies the ability to collaboratively plan, manage, and execute transformation initiatives leading to quantifiable success. The comprehensive solution allows organizations to benefit from centralized and collaborative enhancements, make value-driven decisions, and achieve measurable business value through continuous improvements

 

Access

SAP LeanIX | Cloud Test, Demo, and Development

 

SAP LeanIX is a market leader for enterprise architecture, driving the modernization of IT landscapes and continuous business transformation.

 

 Access

 

 

Explore Business Process Transformation

 

Design, Analyze and Automate improved Processes — Driving Efficiency and Customer Excellence with SAP Signavio. SAP offers a holistic suite of flexible process transformation solutions for customers to transform their business processes end-to-end with SAP Signavio solutions.

 

Access

Partner Delivery Groups

Overview

Register/Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

This group focuses on providing enhanced learnings and periodic updates to the partner community with respect to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition product. Our goal is to facilitate collaboration with your peers and SAP subject matter experts.

 

Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

This private group is focused solely on helping you make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition projects successful. We are dedicated to your success and work to quickly scale the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition product knowledge, and solution lifecycle experiences out to the wider community. We welcome your questions and feedback here and look forward to your active participation.

 

Access

SAP Business Technology Platform

Get up to speed with the latest content and activities in the SAP BTP Partner Group. Don’t miss out the upcoming SAP BTP Partner Community Call on September 20th. Get all the details, save your seat today and share with your network

 

Access

 

SAP AI Core Community

Confidently deploy and integrate AI models designed for SAP applications, cost-efficiently at scale while preserving privacy and compliance.

Access

SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network

In the SAP Ariba and Business Network Delivery Group, we excited to share that we are welcoming our Test, Demonstration & Development Program team, led by Prasanth Rajan, to the SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Partner Delivery Group. This collaboration has allowed us to invite over 300 partner consultants who participate in the Test, Demonstration & Development Program. This initiative is key to enhancing our partner experiences and fostering stronger collaboration.

 

We are extending open invitations to our ISBN partners to ask their how-to questions, with a view to generate constructive and continuous learning in a growing partner group within SAP Community.

Access

SAP Signavio Community

Connect with other SAP Signavio users or experts, find blog posts and ask your questions to the Signavio community.

Access

 

SAP SuccessFactors HXM

SAP SuccessFactors is delivering value across multiple types of Artificial Intelligence (AI).

  • Generative AI: Accelerate HR processes and tasks by quickly generating or summarizing text across the SAP SuccessFactors HXM suite.
  • Conversational AI: Deliver a better employee experience while lowering personnel costs with a natural language processing chatbot.
  • Automation: Lower personnel costs while increasing data accuracy and accelerating repetitive core HR processes.
  • Deep Learning AI: Transform talent development and employee experience with personalized recommendations and deep insights.

Access

SAP Cloud ALM Community

 

Follow the SAP Cloud ALM Community to keep you up to date and ask questions

Access

SAP Customer Experience & CRM

On regular cadences, we partner with internal Customer Experience & CRM subject matter experts to share best practices, new product information and thought leadership through delivery blogs in our partner delivery group. Our blogs are amplified through targeted communications and social media to help encourage engagement amongst partners and internal subject matter experts. Internal subject matter experts are always welcome to contribute blogs amongst any of our other solution partner delivery groups as well.

 

Access

    Within two hours, you will then receive a registration confirmation via e-mail.

Make sure you are logged on to SAP Learning Hub before opening the URL. In case the link doesn’t open, please copy and paste it into browser. If you face any other issues with your login, please contact the help center.

entry.title: Object Page & Table Personalization in WebClient UI apps
entry.content:
WebClient UI serves as a framework for Service Applications in SAP S/4HANA

Personalization offers options on different levels. 

Object Page Personalization

You can personalize object pages using the Personalization dialog, which you open from the Personalization icon on the top right in the title bar of the overview page (or use keyboard shortcut Ctrl + ,). 

Object Page Personalization

In Object Page Personalization, you can adjust the  page as follows: 

  1. Displayed or Hidden Assignment Blocks - in preferred order
  2. Expanded or Collapsed Assignment Blocks

When you make changes on the Object Page, like using drag and drop to change the assignment block order, they are automatically saved to your personalized object page view. 

For more details, check out the Personalizing Pages | SAP Help Portal documentation.  

Table Personalization

You can personalize tables using the Table Personalization dialog, which you open from the Personalization icon on the top right in the title bar of the results list (or use keyboard shortcut Ctrl + ,). 

Table Personalization

In Table Personalization, you can save different personalized Table Views which contains the following: 

  1. Displayed or Hidden Columns - in preferred order
  2. Column Width - defined in pixels or percentage
  3. Fixed Columns - fixed to the left or right of the table 
  4. Filtered Columns - indicator of filtered items
  5. Table Navigation  - scrolling, paging or both and the number of visible rows
  6. Table Sorting - select up to 3 columns to sort in ascending or descending order

When you make changes to the table by clicking on the column header (resizing, changing column order, fixed columns, filtered columns), this is also automatically saved to your personalized table view. For the sorting and filtering settings to persist, you need to ensure that your Personalization Settings are enabled. 

For more details, check out the Table Personalization | SAP Help Portal documentation.

entry.title: Proactively Manage Rapidly Growing Tables using the SAP EarlyWatch Alert
entry.content:
In today's data-driven world, SAP systems continuously process large amounts of data, with some tables growing at a very rapid pace. These rapidly growing tables can pose significant risks if not properly managed, as they may soon hit their technical limits, particularly the 2 billion row threshold in SAP HANA (for more details, see SAP Note 2154870). When a table reaches its capacity, it can result in costly downtime and disruptions to critical business operations. This is where the partitioning of tables becomes a vital technique. Partitioning not only helps in maintaining the performance of large tables but also mitigates the risk of these tables reaching their technical limits, enabling continuous data growth without compromising system stability.

To address this challenge, I am excited to announce the release of a new SAP EarlyWatch Alert (EWA) check designed to specifically monitor and provide best practices for partitioning tables. This new check helps identify up to five of the most critical tables in your system that are (rapidly) approaching their capacity limit and offers recommendations for partitioning.

 

Key Features of the Partitioning Check

The SAP EarlyWatch Alert chapter, titled "Rapidly Growing Tables need to be partitioned," is built with a proactive approach to safeguard your system’s performance. It focuses on tables identified by the SAP EarlyWatch Alert 2 Billion App, which predicts when a table is likely to reach its maximum capacity based on current growth patterns (for more details, see this blog). The partitioning recommendations used are mainly based on the SAP HANA Partitioning note 2044468.

This EWA Check helps by:

  • Identifying the top 5 most critical tables: The check analyzes tables flagged by the 2 Billion App as likely to reach the 2 billion row limit and evaluates the worst-case scenario (worst-case date) for when the table will run out of space.
  • Providing SAP best practices for partitioning: For each critical table, the check lists recommended partitioning techniques based on SAP's best practices, helping you implement the most effective strategy for continued use and system performance optimization.

The new SAP EWA chapter can be found in the SAP EWA report in section "SAP HANA Database - Size and Growth – SAP HANA COLUMN STORE – Rapidly Growing Tables need to be partitioned".

However, there are a few important considerations to keep in mind:

  • The check is limited to up to 5 tables: Only the most critical tables are included, based on their potential to reach the technical limit soon.
  • SAP Best Practices must exist for partitioning: Partitioning recommendations are only provided for tables for which SAP has established best practices. If no such recommendations exist, the table will not appear in this EWA chapter, even if it is critical.
  • Check targets only non-partitioned tables: The focus is on tables that have not yet been partitioned but are rapidly growing and at risk of hitting the technical limit soon.

For tables that are critical but lack partitioning recommendations, you can still monitor their status in the SAP EarlyWatch Alert report under the chapter "SAP HANA Database - Size and Growth – SAP HANA COLUMN STORE – Largest Non-partitioned Column Tables (Records)". This provides valuable visibility into tables that may need alternative solutions or future partitioning options. Additionally, you could also use the 2 Billion App in the SAP EWA Workspace to monitor the (rapidly) growing tables in your system(s) (for more details, see this blog).

The new check also categorizes the urgency of the alert using a color-coded system based on how soon the worst-case date is expected to occur:

Red Alert: If the worst-case date for at least one table is within the next two weeks, the SAP EWA report will generate a decisive red alert, indicating that immediate action is necessary.

Yellow Alert: If the worst-case date falls between two weeks and three months, a yellow alert is generated, advising that partitioning should be planned and executed soon.

Green Alert: If the table’s worst-case date is more than three months away, a green alert is triggered, signalling that there is time to monitor and prepare a partitioning strategy, but the risk is still present.

This tiered approach enables you to focus on the most urgent issues while planning for tables that may become problematic later. Below is an example of how this check would appear in your SAP EarlyWatch Alert report:

You can also use the SAP EarlyWatch Alert Solution Finder to search for (critical) alerts regarding partitioning by entering the text “partitioning” or “rapidly growing tables” in the Enter Search Term field. In this way, you can get an overview for your SAP system landscape and can also directly jump into the corresponding chapter in the SAP EWA report of the affected system (for more details, see this blog).

 

Why Partitioning is Essential for Rapidly Growing Tables

SAP HANA databases are designed to handle vast amounts of data, but even they have limitations. The 2 billion row limit in tables can be a critical threshold, especially for organizations experiencing rapid data growth. When a table hits this limit, it leads to dumps, which could result in business interruptions/downtime.

Partitioning allows you to split large tables into smaller, more manageable pieces (partitions), which optimizes data retrieval and storage. This method helps prevent tables from reaching their capacity, supports continuous system operations, and enhances overall database performance. But also, other Data Volume Management techniques such as deleting or archiving data can prevent tables from reaching the 2 billion limitation.

Without partitioning, systems are at risk of downtime as they hit the technical limit. This downtime can have a significant impact on businesses, potentially causing service interruptions, delayed transactions, and even lost revenue. By adopting partitioning techniques early, based on the proactive insights provided by the SAP EarlyWatch Alert Check, you can avoid these costly outcomes.

 

Stay Ahead with SAP EarlyWatch Alert

The "Rapidly Growing Tables need to be partitioned" chapter provides essential information for any organization looking to maintain optimal SAP HANA performance and avoid critical failures. By monitoring your most vulnerable tables and offering an overview about existing partitioning best practices, this check empowers you to take pre-emptive action, protecting your system from unexpected downtime.

I encourage you to regularly review your SAP EarlyWatch Alert report to stay informed about your system’s health. If you haven't yet, now is the perfect time to look at this new SAP EWA chapter and make sure you’re on top of any tables that are growing too quickly. The insights and best practices provided can be invaluable in ensuring smooth, uninterrupted system performance. Additionally, turning on notifications for the 2 Billion App can be highly beneficial to detect any growing table, regardless of whether partitioning recommendations are available (for more details, see this blog).

Be proactive – read your SAP EarlyWatch Alert report today and on a regular basis to be able to safeguard your system with effective table partitioning strategies.

entry.title: SAP UI Test Automation is Dead as it’s Too Slow - Part Two
entry.content:
In the first part of my article series Was SAP UI Test Automation just killed by SAP API Hub and Solace?, we explored the key factors making SAP UI Test Automation increasingly obsolete. The combination of SAP Business Accelerator Hub and Solace’s event-driven architecture (EDA) introduced powerful alternatives to UI testing by leveraging APIs and real-time event messages. The benefits were clear:

  1. Stability Over Changing Interfaces: Unlike SAP UI testing, API interfaces remain consistent and unaffected by frequent UI updates, making them a more reliable choice.
  2. Broad Participation: API testing enables functional teams, developers, and business users to participate in testing, breaking the dependency on specialized test automation teams.
  3. Simplified Setup: API testing eliminates the need for complex, screen-based test setups.
  4. Cost-Effectiveness: Resource demands for API testing are far lower, leading to significant savings.

However, one major factor we didn’t delve into before was speed. While SAP UI test automation is resource-intensive and complex to set up, it’s also extremely slow in execution, especially when dealing with end-to-end processes across platforms connected to SAP S/4HANA. Let’s explore why SAP UI testing lags so far behind and how API testing has revolutionized speed and efficiency in SAP testing that allows testing a complex SAP Business Processes one hundred times in a matter of 129 seconds only as shown in the Figure 1. 

Figure 1 - testing a complete E2E SAP S/4HANA process 100 times in 129 seconds 

The Drawbacks of SAP UI Test Automation: Setup and Execution Speed

Beyond the challenges we’ve already discussed, SAP UI testing is inefficient by design. Here’s why it slows down both testing and deployment cycles:

  1. Complex Setup: SAP UI test automation requires recording screens across various UI technologies, whether SAP GUI, SAP Fiori, or Web Dynpro. This is labor-intensive and needs to be redone with every update to the UI or business process, making setup slow and costly.

  2. Resource-Heavy Execution: Running a full suite of SAP UI tests demands significant computational resources, often requiring multiple virtual servers to simulate user interactions across different screens. This complexity makes it nearly impossible to run large-scale tests efficiently, limiting how often organizations can execute these tests in agile deployment cycles.

  3. Slow Processing Time: UI-based testing involves replicating user actions, which must wait for each screen to load, process inputs, and complete transitions. This means that even basic tests take longer to execute compared to API-based processes, which bypass the UI entirely.

Speed Advantage of SAP API Testing

In contrast, API testing introduces an entirely different experience when it comes to speed. With API and event-driven architecture, testing bypasses the SAP UI and interacts directly with the business logic and data processing. This shift eliminates many of the inefficiencies inherent in UI testing. Here’s how:

  1. Rapid Execution Without Extra Hardware: Unlike UI testing, which often requires virtual servers and other costly infrastructure, API testing runs smoothly on standard hardware, removing a significant financial and logistical burden.

  2. Blazing-Fast Test Cycles: For example, running an end-to-end SAP Ariba and SAP S/4HANA process 100 times with API testing can take just 129 seconds—an astonishingly short time that UI test automation cannot compete with. This speed enables organizations to test thoroughly and frequently, supporting agile development and faster deployment cycles.

  3. Efficient Resource Utilization: Since API tests only communicate with the business logic layer, they can execute in parallel with minimal resource strain. This allows organizations to maximize testing without overloading their infrastructure, unlike SAP UI tests, which are confined by server availability and loading times.

The End of SAP UI Test Automation?

The clear advantage in setup, resource efficiency, and speed makes SAP API testing the superior approach for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Ariba transformations. The continued reliance on UI testing is becoming increasingly difficult to justify when API testing offers:

  • Incredible Speed: API testing can handle processes in seconds, allowing full-scale testing to occur in the time it would take a single UI test to load.
  • Reliability: API testing bypasses the inconsistencies of changing UI interfaces, enabling a stable and consistent testing environment.
  • Lower Costs: By reducing the need for virtual servers and specialized hardware, API testing frees up valuable resources for other business initiatives.
  • Greater Scalability: With no need for multiple servers or specialized hardware, API tests scale easily across large data sets and complex business processes.

The move towards API and event-driven testing isn’t just an upgrade; it’s a paradigm shift that addresses the limitations of UI-based testing and aligns testing capabilities with modern, agile business needs. By embracing API and EDA-driven testing, organizations can look forward to faster, more efficient, and more cost-effective SAP testing, ensuring their systems meet high standards without sacrificing speed.

Conclusion

As SAP landscapes become more composable and connected, the need for efficient testing methodologies becomes paramount. SAP UI test automation, once a necessary part of testing, now stands in the way of speed and agility. SAP API testing, with its remarkable execution time, efficient resource usage, and low-cost infrastructure, has emerged as the clear replacement.

The time has come for organizations to transition fully from SAP UI to SAP API testing to keep pace with the demands of the modern ERP landscape. The shift not only streamlines testing but ensures that testing supports rather than hinders business agility and innovation.

Further Reference: 

Avoid SAP S/4HANA Project Delays with Third-Party Systems Service Virtualization

entry.title: Deciphering Seamless SAML Single Sign-On: SAP IAS Questions and Answers (part 2)
entry.content:
In my previous blog, I laid the groundwork for configuring Single Sign-On (SSO) using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) and highlighted the crucial role of various Identity Providers (IdPs) in this process. I emphasized IAS's function as a proxy identity provider and discussed how integrating with Azure, Google, AWS, and Active Directory can simplify access to multiple applications within the SAP ecosystem..

In this Part 2 post, I dive into troubleshooting tips and solutions for common SSO and IAS challenges that arise during customer deployments. Presented in a Q&A format, this guide offers practical insights to help streamline your own SSO implementation journey.

Questions and Answers (focus on resolving various issues):

1. When using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) as a hub (proxy) for Identity services, which tenant's SAML 2.0 metadata XML should be imported to the S/4HANA system to establish SAML 2.0 trust?

Answer: To configure SAP IAS as a hub (proxy) for Identity services with S/4HANA, only the SAML XML metadata of the IAS tenant should be uploaded to the S/4HANA system to establish SAML 2.0 trust. Then, the SAML XML response from S/4HANA should be imported back into the IAS application for the appropriate environment tier.

The setup requires:

  1. Uploading the IAS tenant's SAML XML metadata to the S/4HANA system.
  2. Importing the S/4HANA-generated SAML XML metadata response into the corresponding IAS application for that tier, completing the two-way trust for SAML-based Single Sign-On (SSO) setup.

2. How can we resolve the "Domain is not trusted" error message when trying to log in to our SAP Fiori app integrated with SAP IAS?

Answer: Add the domain or the server’s name (the DNS name) you are “connecting to” in your IAS Tenant “Trusted Domains” section as below to prevent that error:

In the IAS tenant go Single Sign-On > Trusted Domains then add your FQDN:

To ensure a smooth setup and resolve potential errors, follow these steps:

  • If required, enable third-party cookies in your browser to prevent issues during authentication.
  • Verify that the correct identity provider (IdP) configuration is set up in your IAS  tenant.

3. Upon entering a corporate email address from our Google tenant when logging in to our ABAP instance via the link deployed in the IAS app, we encountered the error "account.google.com refused to connect." How can we troubleshoot and resolve this issue?

Answer: To troubleshoot and resolve the error, consider the following steps:

  1. Enable Browser Error Logs: If you using google you can use the Chrome Developer Tool to enable error logging. This will provide detailed information about the error and help pinpoint the root cause.
  2. Reproduce the Error: Log in to your ABAP instance via the IAS app using the Google IdP credential,  then observe the Developer Tool for error details.

  3. Check for X-Frame Errors: In similar cases, X-Frame-related issues can originate from the Fiori page, preventing Google from connecting. Look for any "X-Frame-Options" errors in the console, as these may indicate blocked content.

  4.  Install Browser Extensions: If X-Frame issues are confirmed, consider using a Chrome extension that allows cross-frame content, like "Ignore X-Frame Headers." Ensure to enable the extension for "All Sites" to allow content from Google to load correctly.
  5. Verify IAS and Google Configuration: Double-check that IAS is configured correctly to use Google as an Identity Provider (IdP) and that redirect URIs match across configurations. This ensures seamless communication between IAS and Google Workspace.
  6. Test Connection Across Browsers: Sometimes, browser-specific settings may interfere with SSO connections. Test the setup in different browsers to rule out any browser-specific issues.

4. Is it accurate to say ,once we've established trust between the S/4 HANA app in IAS and the ABAP NetWeaver system, then adding a new IdP tenant (domain) in IAS and configuring its SAML2 trust is sufficient to establish SSO? Are there no adjustments required on the S/4 side or any IAS apps associated with the previous IdP to enable this new IdP?

Answer: Yes, that is true, after the trust between IAS and S/4 set up , for any new IdP (a new tenant domain) you only need to create that in IAS corporate IdP section and then only add the IdP email domain in “Conditional Authentication” of the desired App in IAS without touching S/4 or the related app configuration or SAML in IAS.

5. I've set the Fiori link (provisioned through the internal Load Balancer in the S/4 landscape) as the default "URL for Browser Flow" in my IAS S/4 application. Now, when I try to access the WebGUI link directly, it always redirects me to the Fiori page instead. Is there a workaround to access WebGUI without being redirected to Fiori?

Answer: If you want to use WebGUI alongside Fiori access (deployed through your Load Balancer) in an S/4 system with IAS SSO, one approach is to use the ABAP Central Instance (CI) server link in the IAS application. This setup allows you to specify your WebGUI path directly in the URL you access before reaching the Fiori page.

Set the ABAP CI link in IAS’s “URL for Browser Flow.” This bypasses the default Load Balancer (LB) or Web Dispatcher layer, enabling direct access to WebGUI.

This approach provides WebGUI access without depending on Web Dispatcher or any LB in front of Fiori, ensuring direct access when needed. One drawback is that you won’t be able to leverage the benefits of a Web Dispatcher or any front-facing load balancer.

Your “URL for Browser Flow” in IAS APP can be like:
